items: The operating wrapper around agents: filesystem conventions, skills, roster, permissions, receipts, gates, and closeout rules.
A task-specific judgment contract in SKILL.md. It tells the lead agent how to run a workflow without hiding a product inside prose.
A scoped persona or provider lane used for fresh context, implementation, review, or critique.
A deterministic command that must pass before work is treated as trustworthy.
The evidence that proves the requirement, not just a nearby green check.
A sanitized record of who ran, why, what happened, and what the lead accepted.
The configured set of external providers the lead can dispatch and compare.
A repeatable chain such as shape -> deliver -> review -> CI -> QA -> reflect.
An external agent runtime in the roster, such as Claude, Codex, Pi, OpenCode, Grok, Cursor, or Antigravity.
The structured closeout record of a provider attempt: objective, status, evidence, lead verdict, and what was accepted.
concepts.lede: Harness Kit names the parts that need to be assigned, checked, logged, and improved.
concepts.title: The small vocabulary that keeps agent work operational.

cards: Define what good looks like before a model run becomes production behavior.
Record provider attempts, failures, lead verdicts, and accepted synthesis without exposing raw transcripts.
Put humans at the points where policy, money, reputation, or irreversible actions enter the workflow.
Keep tool permissions explicit and narrow enough for the workflow's actual needs.
Know which commit, config, or harness change to revert when behavior regresses.
Name the condition that moves work from agent automation back to human review.
governance.lede: Operational agent work needs evals, traces, approvals, least-privilege tools, rollback, and escalation paths before it earns trust.
governance.title: Controls first, scale second.

home.backlog_copy: The site is generated from live repo sources, including open backlog, so readers can see the operating system and its active edges together.
home.boundary_copy: Harness Kit is implementation infrastructure for technical operators. It is not an admin dashboard, procurement packet, spend-governance product, or executive training surface.
home.boundary_title: What it is, and what it is not
home.lede: Harness Kit gives technical teams the primitives to plan, delegate, review, verify, and improve AI-assisted work without losing the thread.

quickstart.lede: Install the global harness, open this companion, then use the operating loop from a real repo.

steps.detail: Fresh machines can install from GitHub; local checkouts can run ./bootstrap.sh directly.
steps.label: Install the harness

steps.detail: At least one supported harness should show Harness Kit skills after bootstrap completes.
steps.label: Verify discovery

steps.detail: Start with a real workflow or repo change; keep evidence and roster receipts attached to the work.
steps.label: Run the first loop

quickstart.title: Start using Harness Kit

home.sixty_copy: Start with a messy workflow. Shape the work, send it through a provider roster, build with tests, review with independent lanes, prove it with gates, then feed the lesson back into the harness.

start_cards.text: Harnesses, skills, agents, gates, traces, oracles, and rosters in plain language.
start_cards.title: Learn the primitives

start_cards.text: Five concrete paths from raw request to reviewed, verified agent work.
start_cards.title: Walk a workflow

start_cards.text: Generated reference pages for every local skill, agent, and gate.
start_cards.title: Browse the catalog

items.task: Turn a vague idea into buildable work
Check whether a branch is safe to ship
Research an unfamiliar system or market

task_finder.lede: Start from the job you need done. Each path links to a generated source-backed reference page.
task_finder.title: Find the right primitive

home.title: Turn one-off agent runs into repeatable operating loops.

workflows.decision: Pick the workflow with repeated demand, visible friction, and a clear success oracle.
workflows.evidence: Candidate workflow, non-goals, owner, acceptance proof, and failure/escalation conditions.
workflows.failure: If no repeated pain or proof exists, keep researching instead of automating a demo-shaped workflow.
workflows.input: Messy process notes, stakeholder complaints, recent examples, and the current manual handoff.
workflows.summary: Find the narrow repeatable pain before you build the agent.
workflows.title: Choose the first workflow worth automating

workflows.decision: Choose the smallest buildable slice that still makes the desired end state more true.
workflows.evidence: A backlog.d item with goal, problem challenge, design, oracle, non-goals, and related work.
workflows.failure: If the oracle is weak or the audience is wrong, route back to research or positioning before implementation.
workflows.input: A rough request, repo context, constraints, and the nearest live source files.
workflows.summary: Move from ambition to a buildable slice with a real oracle.
workflows.title: Turn a raw idea into a shaped ticket

workflows.decision: Let implementation, review, CI, refactor, and QA each own their part of the evidence loop.
workflows.evidence: Green gate output, review receipts, QA evidence, clean tree, and a delivery brief.
workflows.failure: If any phase cannot produce live evidence, the branch is not merge-ready.
workflows.input: A shaped backlog item with concrete oracle and implementation surface.
workflows.summary: Use the inner loop to turn a shaped ticket into merge-ready work.
workflows.title: Ship a repo change with review and gates

workflows.decision: Prefer a stable checkout and system-wide provider roster over repo-local source bridges.
workflows.evidence: Harness skill links, agent links, roster config, and no duplicate source-repo skill bridges.
workflows.failure: If a harness cannot discover the global install, diagnose bootstrap paths before adding local copies.
workflows.input: A local checkout or the GitHub bootstrap URL.
workflows.summary: Put the shared primitives where every supported harness can find them.
workflows.title: Install the system-wide harness with bootstrap.sh

workflows.decision: Name the automation boundary, approval point, evidence standard, rollback path, and escalation rule.
workflows.evidence: Control gaps, eval/oracle plan, receipt expectations, least-privilege boundary, rollback path, and escalation rule.
workflows.failure: If controls are undefined, keep the workflow in implementation substrate rather than client-facing packaging.
workflows.input: Workflow description, tool permissions, model/provider choices, risk points, and expected operator handoff.
workflows.summary: Decide where the machine stops and the operator steps in.
workflows.title: Audit an AI workflow for evals, monitoring, and approval points
